title: Black Hole Mass Spin Primacy
definition: In a black hole, mass and spin are not properties of an object but the defining parameters of the spacetime geometry itself. A bigger mass means a larger event horizon and greater reach for frame-dragging effects, while angular momentum determines the intensity of the dragging.

proofs.description: Extracted from conversation via CADMIES Harvest Pipeline
proofs.reference: CADMIES Harvest Pipeline v4.0 — raw dump rescue

difficulty_levels.beginner: A black hole's mass sets its size, and its spin sets how strongly it drags space around it.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Mass determines the scale of the event horizon and the reach of frame-dragging. Angular momentum determines the intensity of the dragging effect, not the speed of swirling spacetime.
difficulty_levels.expert: The Kerr metric is parameterized by mass M and specific angular momentum a. Mass primarily sets the horizon radius and overall scale, while a/M determines the degree of frame-dragging intensity. A small, high-mass black hole can exert enormous gravitational influence despite modest angular momentum.

extra_fields.insight: A small, slow-spinning black hole can have outsized influence due to its mass. Size of influence and intensity of spin are distinct parameters.
